acceptance.assertion: Document which attacker or accident scenarios signing would mitigate and which it would not.
Compare local key signing, Sigstore-style approaches and repository-bound hash manifests.
Produce a concrete recommendation: implement, defer, or reject, with rollout cost and operational burden.
Does not weaken current head/diff binding or make unsigned current evidence silently acceptable if signing is adopted.

goal: Evaluate whether Grabowski review evidence should be signed or otherwise provenance-bound beyond head/diff SHA-256 binding.

metadata.reason: Cryptographic evidence signing is potentially useful but too large and policy-heavy for the current gate hardening patch.

title: Evaluate signed review evidence
